case_title: EDWIN CAAMAL QUIJADA VS. FORD MOTOR COMPANY, A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL

docket_entries.description: DECLARATION OF CHEN FEI LIU IN SUPPORT OF DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 100219119) FILED BY DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY
NOTICE OF WITHDRAWAL OF MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION (TRANSACTION ID # 74614319) FILED BY DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY

LAW & MOTION/DISCOVERY, DEPT. 301, AS TO APRIL-09-2025 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF EDWIN QUIJADA'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEYS FEES, COSTS AND EXPENSES; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF EDWIN CAAMAL QUIJADA MOVES FOR AN ORDER ENTERING JUDGMENT AGAINST DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY (FORD) AND IN FAVOR OF PLAINTIFF FOR ATTORNEYS' FEES UNDER THE LODESTAR METHOD IN THE AMOUNT OF $21,397.00, FOR A LODESTAR ENHANCEMENT OF 0.5 IN THE AMOUNT OF $10,698.50 (WHICH TOTALS $32,095.50 AS THE ATTORNEYS' FEES), AND FOR COSTS TOTALING $2,832.25. THE TOTAL AMOUNT REQUESTED BY THIS MOTION IS $34,927.75. THE COURT GRANTS IN PART AND DENIES IN PART THE REQUESTED RELIEF (SEE WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING). COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING, WITH A COVER LETTER REFLECTING COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (301/CVA)

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-25-2025 Off Calendar

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-24-2025 Continued To Jul-01-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Otice Of Motion & Motion For Attorneys Fees, Costs And Expenses
Off Calendar Apr-09-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF EDWIN QUIJADA's Motion For Attorneys Fees, Costs And Expenses.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Edwin Caamal Quijada moves for an order entering judgment against defendant Ford Motor Company (Ford) and in favor of Plaintiff for attorneys' fees under the lodestar method in the amount of $21,397.00, for a lodestar enhancement of 0.5 in the amount of $10,698.50 (which totals $32,095.50 as the attorneys' fees), and for costs totaling $2,832.25. The total amount requested by this motion is $34,927.75. The court grants in part and denies in part the requested relief (see word minutes for complete ruling).

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling, with a cover letter reflecting compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Oct-01-2024 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Apr-29-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION .
Off Calendar Sep-30-2024 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Entry Of Protective Order To Govern Production Of Confidential Materials; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Sep-09-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY'S Motion For Entry Of Protective Order To Govern Production Of Confidential Materials.

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):

Debra Reed, Esq., of Knight Law Group LLP, (310) 552-2250, for Plaintiff.

Jake Moawad, Esq., of Mortenson Taggart Adams LLP, (949) 774-2224, for Defendant.

Counsel for both parties stipulate to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The parties authorize the clerk to execute the formal stipulation on their behalf.

The subject matter is discussed. At the conclusion of discussion, the matter is taken under submission.

Judge Pro Tem: Philip Ward. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (525/JPT)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-23-2024 Set For Trial On Nov-04-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-11-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom):

Thach Tran, Esq., of KNIGHT LAW GROUP, LLP, (310) 552-2250, for Plaintiff.

Teresa Alarcon, Esq., of MORTENSON TAGGART ADAMS LLP, (949) 774-2224, for Defendant.

RE: DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

Ruling is ARGUED. At conclusion of argument, the court adopts its tentative ruling ass follows: Defendant Ford Motor Company's demurrer to the concealment cause of action is overruled. Plaintiff alleges a sufficient relationship between plaintiff and defendant for the concealment claim. (Dhital v. Nissan N. Am. Inc. (2022) 84 Cal.App.5th 828, 844 [allegations that plaintiff purchased vehicle from Nissan dealership, that Nissan issued a warranty, and dealership was agent of Nissan were sufficient to show relationship with manufacturer for concealment claim] (review granted); CRC 8.1115(e)(1) [opinion may be cited for persuasive value where review granted].) Plaintiff's active concealment and exclusive knowledge allegations are sufficient to support the concealment cause of action. (Heliotis v. Schuman (1986) 181 Cal.App.3d 646, 651 [party may plead concealment claim based on active concealment or exclusive knowledge where no fiduciary relationship exists]; Complaint, pars. 15-45.) In Falk v. Gen. Motors Corp. (N.D. Cal. 2007) 496 F.Supp.2d 1088, 1096-97, the court found a duty to disclose based on "exclusive knowledge" where the manufacturer had superior knowledge of a defect that was not readily apparent. The court noted that customers are not required to conduct an internet search prior to purchasing a car. (Id. at 1097.) In any event, plaintiff also alleges active concealment. (Complaint, par. 46.) The court rejects defendant's argument that the concealment claim is not alleged with the requisite particularity. Unlike an intentional misrepresentation claim, a plaintiff alleging a concealment claim likely has less information and the specificity requirement is "relaxed when it is apparent from the allegations that the defendant necessarily possesses knowledge of the facts." (Quelimane Co. v. Steward Title Guaranty Co. (1998) 19 Cal.4th 26, 47; see also Alfaro v. Community Housing Imp. System & Planning Ass'n, Inc. (2009) 171 Cal.App.4th 1356, 1384.)

The court rejects defendant's argument that the economic loss rule bars the concealment claim. (Dhital, 84 Cal.App.5th at 840 ["we conclude plaintiffs' claim for fraudulent inducement by concealment is not subject to demurrer on the ground it is barred by the economic loss rule."]; Vega v. Jones, Day, Reavis & Pogue (2004) 121 Cal.App.4th 282, 291 ["Active concealment or suppression of facts by a nonfiduciary 'is the equivalent of a false representation, i.e., actual fraud.'
[Citation.]"].)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr. Clerk: Robert Woods. Court Reporter: Raquel Sharp, T: 925.226.8222, E: raquel@transcriptlegal.com. (302/RBU)
